name: Manzanares Canal Greenway
name_original: Madrid Río y Parque Lineal del Manzanares
description: Cycle the easy Manzanares Canal Greenway, a 17.6-mile (28.3 km) route along the Manzanares River, perfect for a relaxed ride.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Area Metropolitana De Madrid offers diverse landscapes, from the challenging mountain passes of the Sierra de Guadarrama to tranquil river basins and expansive urban parks. The region features extensive greenways, such as the Anillo Verde Ciclista, and riverside trails along the Manzanares River. Cyclists can explore varied terrains including pine forests, olive groves, and vineyards, with routes often utilizing quiet roads and converted railway tracks. Best touring cycling routes around Area Metropolitana De Madrid The most popular touring cycling route is Anillo Verde Ciclista – complete loop from Casa de Campo, a 40.6 miles (65.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 59 minutes to complete, circling the city through urban and park areas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Manzanares Canal Greenway, an easy 17.6 miles (28.3 km) path. This route follows the Manzanares River, offering flat terrain suitable for relaxed rides. Local touring cyclists also love the Arco Verde: Madrid Airport Loop, a 23.1 miles (37.2 km) trail leading through the Arco Verde network, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Area Metropolitana De Madrid is defined by mountain passes, river basins, and urban parks, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Area Metropolitana De Madrid are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3700 reviews. More than 41000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Area Metropolitana De Madrid's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Area Metropolitana De Madrid?
A: The Area Metropolitana De Madrid offers a diverse range of terrains for touring cyclists. You can find challenging mountain passes in the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/strong&gt;, tranquil greenways, and expansive urban parks. Routes often utilize quiet roads, converted railway tracks (Vías Verdes), and riverside trails along the Manzanares River. Expect a mix of pine forests, olive groves, and vineyards, with options ranging from flat paths to moderately steep gradients.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are many easy routes perfect for beginners and families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962537909/manzanares-canal-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares Canal Greenway&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 28.3 km path that follows the Manzanares River, offering flat terrain ideal for relaxed rides. The &lt;strong&gt;Casa de Campo Park&lt;/strong&gt; also provides numerous internal routes suitable for leisurely rides, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962569514/casa-de-campo-circular-from-metro-batan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Campo – circular from Metro Batán&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.1 km long.

Q: What natural features can I explore while touring cycling in the region?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through the rugged peaks and deep valleys of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Guadarrama&lt;/strong&gt;, explore the protected habitats of the &lt;strong&gt;Monte de El Pardo&lt;/strong&gt;, or enjoy the vast green spaces of &lt;strong&gt;Casa de Campo Park&lt;/strong&gt;. The Manzanares River and several reservoirs like El Pardo and Atazar also offer scenic routes. You might even encounter impressive gorges such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5123913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarama Cliffs&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4232941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rivas Cliffs&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The CiclaMadrid network, for example, passes through historic cities like Aranjuez, Alcalá de Henares, and San Lorenzo de El Escorial, and charming villages such as Chinchón. Within Madrid, cycling tours can take you past landmarks like the Royal Palace, Almudena Cathedral, Plaza Mayor, and Retiro Park. You can also find interesting man-made monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3440393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Retiro’s Crystal Palace&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/317503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Gasco Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How accessible are the touring cycling routes by public transport?
A: Many routes in the Area Metropolitana De Madrid are well-connected to public transport. The &lt;strong&gt;Anillo Verde Ciclista&lt;/strong&gt;, for instance, circles the city and is easily accessible from various metro and train stations. Routes starting from urban parks like Casa de Campo, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959421495/anillo-verde-ciclista-complete-loop-from-casa-de-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anillo Verde Ciclista – complete loop from Casa de Campo&lt;/a&gt;, often have convenient access points near public transport hubs like Metro Batán.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Area Metropolitana De Madrid?
A: The Area Metropolitana De Madrid offers a year-round cycling climate. Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) are particularly pleasant, with mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, especially in July and August, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter cycling is also possible, with generally mild conditions, though mountain areas like the Sierra de Guadarrama may experience colder temperatures.

Q: Are there many circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, the region offers numerous circular touring cycling routes, which are great for exploring without needing a return trip. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959421495/anillo-verde-ciclista-complete-loop-from-casa-de-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anillo Verde Ciclista – complete loop from Casa de Campo&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962483991/arco-verde-madrid-airport-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arco Verde: Madrid Airport Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962366281/soto-de-vinuelas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soto de Viñuelas Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Many regional parks also feature excellent circular options, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959859539/parque-regional-del-curso-medio-del-guadarrama-circular-from-alcorcon-by-the-puente-de-hierro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Regional del Curso Medio del Guadarrama – circular from Alcorcón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Area Metropolitana De Madrid?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3700 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain passes to tranquil river basins, and the extensive network of well-maintained routes. The ability to combine city sightseeing with natural cycling experiences is also a frequently highlighted positive aspect.

Q: Are there any specific routes that are particularly good for long-distance touring?
A: For longer distances, the comprehensive &lt;strong&gt;CiclaMadrid&lt;/strong&gt; network offers various routes, including the Grand Tour, which can span approximately 666 kilometers with significant ascent, taking cyclists through historic cities, picturesque villages, and varied landscapes. While not a single route in our guide, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959421495/anillo-verde-ciclista-complete-loop-from-casa-de-campo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anillo Verde Ciclista&lt;/a&gt; provides a substantial 65 km loop around the city, which can be combined with other routes for extended rides.

Q: Can I find routes that include viewpoints or places to stop for refreshments?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Area Metropolitana De Madrid offer scenic viewpoints and pass through towns or villages where you can find cafes and restaurants. The &lt;strong&gt;CiclaMadrid&lt;/strong&gt; network is designed to take you through charming villages, ensuring opportunities for refreshments. Routes in urban parks like &lt;strong&gt;Casa de Campo&lt;/strong&gt; also have facilities nearby. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962665210/parque-regional-curso-medio-del-guadarrama-circular-from-villanueva-de-la-canada-by-the-castillo-de-aulencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Regional Curso Medio del Guadarrama – circular from Villanueva de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt; offers both natural beauty and proximity to amenities.

Q: Are there routes that explore the regional parks around Madrid?
A: Yes, several routes delve into the beautiful regional parks. You can explore the &lt;strong&gt;Parque Regional del Curso Medio del Guadarrama&lt;/strong&gt; with routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959859539/parque-regional-del-curso-medio-del-guadarrama-circular-from-alcorcon-by-the-puente-de-hierro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;circular from Alcorcón by the Puente de Hierro&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962665210/parque-regional-curso-medio-del-guadarrama-circular-from-villanueva-de-la-canada-by-the-castillo-de-aulencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;circular from Villanueva de la Cañada by the Castillo de Aulencia&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Parque Regional del Sureste&lt;/strong&gt; also offers routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962585481/parque-regional-del-sureste-circular-by-rivas-and-velilla-de-san-antonio-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;circular by Rivas and Velilla de San Antonio&lt;/a&gt;, providing diverse natural experiences.
tips:
Highly recommended for cycling or going with children, although some days the area can be very crowded.
The Manzanares is not only Madrid Río, be careful. The part known as the Manzanares Linear Park is also worth visiting.
https://bigjoe.es

The Puente de los Franceses (French Bridge) is an imposing historic arched bridge, built of brick and granite, that spans the Manzanares River with five distinctive semicircular arches. It was erected between 1860 and 1862 and owes its name to the French engineers who designed it.

The construction of the Madrid Río park and the burying of the M30 have restored its splendor to this bridge, which is a jewel of the Spanish Renaissance.

The Segovia bridge was built by the same architect as the Monastery of San Lorenzo de El Escorial under the reign of Philip II in the 16th century. The ponds that were built where the M30 used to pass are home to native aquatic fauna and flora. The reflection of the Palacio de Oriente and the Almudena Cathedral in the water creates a perfect scene to immortalize with a photo.
Very cool bridge in the middle of Madrid. Two spirals in each other.
Excellent to enjoy it at 1st hour in the morning

There is always some interesting cultural activity going on.
You can enter with the bicycle and take a walk visiting the different areas.
There are bars and a bike rental and spare parts shop (check timetables).

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling on the Manzanares Canal Greenway?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied cycling experience. You'll find extensive stretches of paved roads, asphalt, and dedicated cycleways, making much of it smooth and easy to ride. However, some sections may include natural paths, gravel, or unpaved segments, occasionally requiring you to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy picturesque and diverse scenery. The route follows the Manzanares River, passing through urban parks like Casa de Campo, Parque del Oeste, and the Manzanares Linear Park. Expect a mix of natural landscapes, historical bridges such as Puente de Toledo and the Bridge of the French, modern sculptures, and the transformed riverbanks of Madrid Río with tree-lined promenades and cafés. You might also spot wetlands and various bird species.

faqs.question: Is the Manzanares Canal Greenway suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, many parts of the greenway are considered easy, flat, and leafy, making them pleasant for family outings and accessible to cyclists of all skill levels. The route often features rest spaces, water fountains, and play areas, enhancing its family-friendly appeal.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Manzanares Canal Greenway?
faqs.answer: The route features several notable highlights. You'll cycle along the Manzanares River, pass the historic Bridge of the French, and get a View of the Segovia Bridge from Madrid Río. Other highlights include the modern Arganzuela Footbridge, the expansive Manzanares Linear Park, and the cultural center Matadero Madrid.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other cycling routes in Madrid?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Manzanares Canal Greenway is a crucial part of Madrid's Green Cycling Ring (Anillo Verde Ciclista), a large 64-km circular route connecting the city's natural spaces. This integration allows for longer and more varied cycling tours around the city. It also intersects with routes like Ruta Verde 3.1 Canal del Manzanares and Ruta Verde 05 Madrid Río.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for cycling, with comfortable temperatures and clear skies. Summers can be hot, while winters are typically mild but can be chilly.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Manzanares Canal Greenway?
faqs.answer: While many urban parks in Madrid are dog-friendly, specific regulations for dogs on the cycling paths of the Manzanares Canal Greenway and Madrid Río can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash and be mindful of other cyclists and pedestrians. Check local signage for any specific restrictions in certain areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle here?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Manzanares Canal Greenway or access the Madrid Río park area. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trail using public transport?
faqs.answer: The Manzanares Canal Greenway, particularly the Madrid Río section, is well-integrated into the city. You can find various parking options in the surrounding neighborhoods, though urban parking can be challenging. For public transport, numerous metro and bus lines serve the areas adjacent to the river, offering convenient access points along the route.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or places to eat along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, especially along the transformed riverbanks of Madrid Río, you'll find various cafés and establishments where you can stop for refreshments or a meal. The area is vibrant and offers opportunities to relax and enjoy the atmosphere.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a touring bicycle trip on this greenway?
faqs.answer: Given the route's urban and park settings, pack essentials like water, snacks, sun protection (sunscreen, hat), and basic bike repair tools. A camera is also a good idea to capture the scenic views and landmarks. While many sections are easy, comfortable cycling attire is always recommended.

waypoints.name: View of the Segovia Bridge from Madrid Río
